2605.09505.title: EpiGraph: Building Generalists for Evidence-Intensive Epilepsy Reasoning in the Wild
2605.09505.paper_summary: Epilepsy diagnosis and treatment require evidence-intensive reasoning across heterogeneous clinical knowledge, including biosignal patterns, genetic mechanisms, pharmacogenomics, treatment strategies, and patient outcomes. In this work, we present \textsc{EpiGraph}, a large-scale epilepsy knowledge graph and benchmark for evaluating knowledge-augmented clinical reasoning. \textsc{EpiGraph} integrates 48,166 peer-reviewed papers and seven clinical resources into a heterogeneous graph containing 24,324 entities and 32,009 evidence-grounded triplets across five clinical layers. Built upon this graph, \textsc{EpiBench} defines five clinically motivated tasks spanning clinical decision-making, EEG report generation, pharmacogenomic precision medicine, treatment recommendation, and deep research planning. We evaluate six LLMs under both standard and Graph-RAG settings. Results show that integrating \textsc{EpiGraph} consistently improves performance across all tasks, with the largest gains observed in pharmacogenomic reasoning (+30--41\%). Our findings demonstrate that structured epilepsy knowledge substantially enhances evidence-grounded clinical reasoning and provides a practical benchmark framework for evaluating knowledge-augmented LLMs in real-world neurological settings. Our code is available at: https://github.com/LabRAI/EEG-KG.
2605.09505.paper_summary_zh: 癲癇的診斷和治療需要在異質的臨床知識中進行證據密集型推理，包括生物信號模式、遺傳機制、藥物基因組學、治療策略和患者結果。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們介紹了\textsc{EpiGraph}，一個大規模的癲癇知識圖譜和用於評估知識增強臨床推理的基準。<lb/> \textsc{EpiGraph}整合了48,166篇經過同行評審的論文和七個臨床資源，形成一個包含24,324個實體和32,009個基於證據的三元組的異質圖，涵蓋五個臨床層面。<lb/>基於這個圖，\textsc{EpiBench}定義了五個臨床驅動的任務，涵蓋臨床決策、腦電圖報告生成、藥物基因組學精準醫療、治療建議和深度研究規劃。<lb/>我們在標準和Graph-RAG設置下評估了六個LLM。<lb/>結果顯示，整合\textsc{EpiGraph}在所有任務中一致性地提高了性能，藥物基因組學推理的增益最大（+30--41%）。<lb/>我們的發現表明，結構化的癲癇知識顯著增強了基於證據的臨床推理，並提供了一個實用的基準框架，用於評估在現實神經學環境中增強知識的LLM。<lb/>我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/LabRAI/EEG-KG。
2605.09505.author: Yuyang Dai et.al.
2605.09505.authors: Yuyang Dai, Zheng Chen, Jathurshan Pradeepkumar, Yasuko Matsubara, Jimeng Sun, Yasushi Sakurai, Yushun Dong
